title: veronica multifida var. bihariensis
intro: Veronica multifida var. bihariensis, also known as Bihari Veronica, is a plant species belonging to the Veronica genus, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as speedwells. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Bihari Veronica include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or used in the form of powder or capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
